Package javashooter.collider

Class CircleCollider

java.lang.Object
javashooter.collider.Collider
javashooter.collider.CircleCollider
public class CircleCollider extends Collider
Collider for a circular object.
Author:
geppe

  • Method Details

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Collider

    • checkCollisionCircCirc

      public boolean checkCollisionCircCirc(Collider _c2) throws Exception
      need not consider rotations here since circles are rotation invariant
      Throws:
      Exception

    • collidesWith

      public boolean collidesWith(Collider other)
      example of how to implement own colliders. This one check for collisions with instances of itself. For this, the code tries to cast the parameter other to CircleCollider. If it is possible, collision is evaluated. If not, an exception is raised to signal that thos javashooter.collider type cannot be handled.
      Specified by:
      collidesWith in class Collider
      Parameters:
      other - subclass of Collider
      Returns:
      false or true
      Throws:
      RuntimeException - if other cannot be handled

    • computeContactPoint

      public void computeContactPoint(double[] vector2CenterIntrinsic, double[] cp)
      Computes intersection point in intrinsic coordinates, assuming that there *is* a collision. For this, center of object is trafo'ed to intrinsic CS, and obj speed is appropriately rotated. Returns intersection point in intrinsic CS.
      Specified by:
      computeContactPoint in class Collider
      Parameters:
      objCenter -
      dir -
      cp -
